title: Minimum Difficulty of a Job Schedule

testcases.input: jobDifficulty = [6,5,4,3,2,1], d = 2 Output: 7 Explanation: First day you can finish the first 5 jobs, total difficulty = 6. Second day you can finish the last job, total difficulty = 1. The difficulty of the schedule = 6 + 1 = 7
testcases.expectedOutput: 7 Explanation: First day you can finish the first 5 jobs, total difficulty = 6. Second day you can finish the last job, total difficulty = 1. The difficulty of the schedule = 6 + 1 = 7

testcases.input: jobDifficulty = [9,9,9], d = 4 Output: -1 Explanation: If you finish a job per day you will still have a free day. you cannot find a schedule for the given jobs.
testcases.expectedOutput: -1 Explanation: If you finish a job per day you will still have a free day. you cannot find a schedule for the given jobs.

testcases.input: jobDifficulty = [1,1,1], d = 3 Output: 3 Explanation: The schedule is one job per day. total difficulty will be 3.
testcases.expectedOutput: 3 Explanation: The schedule is one job per day. total difficulty will be 3.
